channel.title: Comments for page "My Dearest Mary"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "My Dearest Mary"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The wording is a little strange in places, but you drove that M. Night Shyamalan twist home. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I didn't see the twist coming until the end, and my first thought was that he's lucky not to be a rabbit or boar, or else it might have been his execution. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Logic be damned, I like this. I didn't realize the twist until the end, and it made me laugh. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In regards to location and rabies, I'm willing to compromise with logic and reason in favour of narrative technique (there's nothing more Africana than temperate woodland!), and I never stated there wasn't a noble leading the mission.

But I understand the headcanon issue as you are the author, and it's your vote to spend.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No vote for now, for the reasons Smapti mentioned. Just needs a little work to push it into upvote territory.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't know about this one. It's nice to see something I created popping up in tales, but this clashes with my headcanon too much. Florida is pretty far afield for Eugenio's missionaries (they live in the Pacific Northwest), there should be a member of the noble caste leading the expedition, and the animals really ought to have heard of rabies before.

No vote for now, I need to let it percolate for awhile.
